caps.reason: You cannot be placed abroad without a passport, so your readiness is held at needs prep until you have one in hand. It is the first thing to sort, and SA passport turnaround is usually a few weeks.

A completed bachelor degree is a hard visa requirement for China, South Korea, the Gulf and Vietnam, so without one those markets are closed and your readiness is held at needs prep. Cambodia and online teaching remain open, or finishing a degree unlocks the main markets.

Almost no legitimate employer abroad will hire you without a TEFL or TESOL certificate, so your readiness is held at needs prep until you commit to one. A 120-hour-plus course is the baseline; CELTA or Trinity opens the best-paid roles.

reason.CELTA (Cambridge): CELTA is the premium credential and opens the best-paid Korea, Gulf and Vietnam roles.
reason.Trinity CertTESOL: Trinity CertTESOL is treated on par with CELTA by reputable employers worldwide.
reason.Ofqual-regulated Level 5 Diploma: An Ofqual-regulated Level 5 diploma is widely accepted and clears most strict visa checks.
reason.Online TEFL (120hr+): A 120-hour-plus certificate meets the market standard and qualifies you for most China, Vietnam and Thailand jobs.
reason.Online TEFL (under 120hr): A sub-120-hour course falls below the market standard, so top up to at least 120 hours before applying.
reason.None yet — planning to get one: No certificate yet is a real blocker; book a 120-hour-plus course now as it is the baseline hiring requirement.
reason.None — not planning: Without a TEFL or TESOL certificate almost no legitimate employer abroad will hire you.

reason.Bachelor (any field): A completed bachelor degree satisfies the hard visa requirement in China, Vietnam, the Gulf and South Korea.
reason.Honours / Postgrad Diploma: Your degree clears the visa bar in every major TEFL market and helps with international-school roles.
reason.Master / PhD: A postgraduate degree clears every visa requirement and qualifies you for university and senior posts.
reason.Diploma (3-year): A diploma is not a bachelor degree, so China, Korea and the Gulf visa routes are closed; Cambodia and some private options remain.
reason.Matric only: Without a degree the main work-visa markets are closed; Cambodia or online teaching are the realistic routes.
reason.Currently studying: Finish and graduate first, as the degree certificate is a hard visa requirement in the best-paid markets.

reason.None: No teaching experience yet is workable for entry-level hagwon and language-centre jobs but limits the better posts.
reason.Under 1 year: Under a year of teaching is enough for entry-level roles; a first overseas contract builds the rest.
reason.1–2 years: One to two years of experience meets the bar for most mainstream TEFL employers.
reason.3–5 years: Three to five years makes you a strong candidate for selective schools and government programmes.
reason.5+ years: Five years or more positions you for senior, university and international-school roles.

reason.true: Prior overseas teaching reassures employers and strengthens every application.
reason.false: No overseas experience yet is normal for first-time teachers and not a barrier to entry-level roles.

reason.Native / first language: English as your first language meets the native-speaker preference of Korea, the Gulf and most premium employers.
reason.Fluent: Fluent English is accepted by most employers, though a few native-only programmes may still screen you out.
reason.Conversational: Conversational English is a blocker for teaching the language; build towards fluency and a proof-of-proficiency test.

reason.Valid — 2+ years remaining: Your passport meets the validity most TEFL work visas require.
reason.Valid — under 2 years remaining: Renew your passport soon, as most work visas need at least two years remaining.
reason.Expired: An expired passport must be renewed before any visa application can start.
reason.No passport: No passport is the first blocker; apply immediately as it gates every other step.

reason.Current (within 6 months): A current SAPS police clearance meets the visa-ready bar for every TEFL destination.
reason.Older than 6 months: Your police clearance is over six months old and most visas require a fresh one.
reason.None: No police clearance yet; apply now as SAPS turnaround runs several weeks and every TEFL visa needs it.

reason.Under R30k: Below the R40k to R80k usually needed upfront before your first overseas salary lands.
reason.R30k – R70k: Covers a lean relocation if your employer reimburses flights and provides housing.
reason.R70k – R150k: A comfortable buffer for flights, deposits and living costs until your first pay cheque.
reason.R150k+: A strong financial position that covers relocation and any unexpected delays.
reason.Prefer not to say: Budget not declared; share it privately with us if it shapes your destination options.

reason.Young Learners (YL): Young-learner training is in heavy demand across Asian language centres and hagwons.
reason.Business English: Business English specialists earn a premium with corporate and adult-learner employers.
reason.IELTS / exam prep: Exam-prep skills are sought after and let you charge more for results-driven classes.
reason.Online teaching: Online-teaching training adds a flexible income stream alongside a classroom contract.
reason.One-to-one tutoring: Tutoring experience helps with private students but carries less weight than classroom specialisms.
reason.None: No specialism recorded; a young-learner or exam-prep add-on would lift your demand and pay.

rules.empty_reason: No specialism training recorded. A young-learner or exam-prep credential is the easiest way to raise your demand and starting salary.

ranges.reason: No dependants makes you the easiest profile to relocate, with no dependant visas to arrange.
One dependant adds a visa step and cost but is manageable in most TEFL markets.
Two dependants raise visa, housing and schooling costs, so favour family-friendly destinations.
Three or more dependants make relocation costly and complex; specialist guidance is needed.

reason.As soon as possible: An ASAP timeline lines up with the rolling hagwon and language-centre hiring cycles.
reason.Within 6 months: A six-month horizon is realistic once your certificate and documents are ready.
reason.Within 1 year: A year gives you room to certify, legalise documents and process the visa without rushing.
reason.Just exploring: Exploring only means no urgency, so you can plan your route and budget at your own pace.
